query: Instead of using the back-projection inpainting method, I want to use the SDXL inpainting model directly on the masked areas, but I also want the ControlNet to guide the inpainting after the fact rather than condition it initially. Can you restructure the workflow to do that?

desired.outcome: A working workflow where the inpainting is performed by the SDXL model directly on masked input, with ControlNet applied as a post-inpainting refinement guide, preserving the original upscaling and detail enhancement stages.
desired.quality: All connections are fully wired: the mask and image correctly feed the inpainting model, ControlNet receives the inpainted result (or appropriate intermediate), and the output routes to upscaling and Detail Daemon without dangling nodes.

_tags.author_rationale: This query forces the agent to re-architect the core inpainting approach and ControlNet conditioning flow, testing deep understanding of the workflow's structure and the interaction between back-projection vs. direct inpainting.
